steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence uses present-tense verbs throughout (“are,” “travel,” “pack”) to describe the Sherpas’ recurring activities. The blank is the second verb in a compound predicate joined by “and,” so it must match the present tense established by “pack.”

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘get’: Simple present tense, consistent with “pack” and the other present-tense verbs. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘had gotten’: Past perfect tense — signals an action completed before another past event, contradicting the present-tense context. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘got’: Simple past tense, inconsistent with the present-tense verbs used throughout the sentence. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘were getting’: Past progressive tense, inconsistent with the present-tense context. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
